workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just came out of security posture with Wide World Importers. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 16:51 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers security posture

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 09:11 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with security posture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 15:42 EST
Subject: Re: security posture Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Wide World Importers: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-I trap (Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-I failure mode (Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-I -- Misattributes a statement to wrong thread participant
